summary: PR 2650 linked src/melee/lb/lb_00CE.c by moving the object from NonMatching to Matching, updating lb_00CE .sdata2 symbol metadata from global lb_804D-address names to local @ labels, adding an sdata2_order helper that references float/double constants, and simplifying squared-distance expressions in lb_8000D148. The bot report records 2 new matches: lb_00CE .sdata2 to 100% and lb_8000D148 to 100%.

key_files.role: Main decomp source updated with include changes, an sdata2_order helper, and lb_8000D148 expression changes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report showing lb_8000D148 99.00% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Symbol metadata changed lb_00CE .sdata2 constants from global address-style names to local @ labels.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report showing main/melee/lb/lb_00CE .sdata2 40.28% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Object status changed from NonMatching to Matching for melee/lb/lb_00CE.c.

what_changed.text: Marked Object(Matching, "melee/lb/lb_00CE.c") in configure.py, replacing Object(NonMatching, ...).

Renamed ten .sdata2 entries at 0x804D7A68 through 0x804D7AA4 from global lb_804D-address symbols to local @15, @16, @17, @18, @20, @22, @34, @35, @36, and @37 labels while preserving size, alignment, and float/double data annotations.
Added static void sdata2_order(void) referencing M_PI_2, M_PI, 1.0f, 2.0f, S32_TO_F32, 0.0f, 0.5, 3.0, 0.00001f, and -0.00001f.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: ribbanya review body: "This is the simplest way to force float order."

what_changed.text: Changed math_ppc include style from "MSL/math_ppc.h" to <math_ppc.h> and added "placeholder.h".
In lb_8000D148, removed diff_02_x/diff_02_y/diff_12_x/diff_12_y temporaries and computed squared distances inline from point coordinates.
Bot report recorded overall progress: matched code +420 bytes, linked code +1180 bytes, matched data +64 bytes, linked data +64 bytes, with two new matches for lb_00CE .sdata2 and lb_8000D148.

smart_moves.text: Kept data ordering work narrow to the lb_00CE .sdata2 range and paired it with symbol metadata changes rather than broad data-section churn.
Used a small source expression rewrite in lb_8000D148 to close the remaining function diff instead of larger control-flow restructuring.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For this lb_00CE link, text and data closure were coupled: the object was only moved to Matching after both lb_8000D148 and the TU .sdata2 reached 100% in the bot report.

When local .sdata2 constants are exposed as address-style globals, symbol metadata may need correction to local @ labels if the diff evidence shows those constants belong as local float/double literals for the TU.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt changes lb_804D7A68-lb_804D7AA4 to @ labels

naming_conventions.convention: For this PR's lb_00CE .sdata2 constants, local @NN labels replaced global lb_804D-address names at 0x804D7A68 through 0x804D7AA4.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Added an unused static sdata2_order function with void-cast references to constants/macros to force .sdata2 float/double ordering.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: ribbanya review body: "This is the simplest way to force float order."
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a narrow historical tactic from the PR, not a global style endorsement; current standards require data-order and tactic use to be evidence-backed and scoped.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline squared-distance arithmetic can affect codegen: lb_8000D148 matched after replacing temporary diff variables with repeated coordinate subtraction expressions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR evidence shows the result for this function only; do not generalize without objdiff evidence.

review_feedback.interpretation: No concrete technical request is present in the loaded text.

review_feedback.body: This is the simplest way to force float order.
review_feedback.interpretation: Reviewer text directly describes the float-order forcing tactic, but the loaded slice does not include inline comments or final approval rationale.
No technical feedback body is available.

follow_up_queries: ribbanya "simplest way to force float order"

evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report and review records
notes: Diff and bot report strongly support changed files, object status, symbol metadata edits, and reported match progress.
PR body is empty, and no inline review comments are loaded.
Review records include dismissed reviews; only one review body contains technical wording about forcing float order.
The loaded evidence does not include local build logs, objdiff output, or final reviewer approval rationale beyond the bot report and merge metadata.
The sdata2_order tactic conflicts with cautionary global standards if generalized; it is recorded here as historical PR evidence only, not as an accepted global standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2650 used sdata2_order to force lb_00CE float order
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2650, lb_00CE added a static sdata2_order function with void-cast references to constants/macros, and a reviewer described it as "the simplest way to force float order." The bot report showed main/melee/lb/lb_00CE .sdata2 improved from 40.28% to 100.00%. Treat as a narrow historical tactic, not a blanket style rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: lb_8000D148 matched after inline squared-distance rewrite
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2650 rewrote lb_8000D148 squared-distance calculations from temporary diff variables to inline repeated coordinate subtraction expressions; the bot report showed lb_8000D148 moved from 99.00% to 100.00%.

source_update_candidates.title: lb_00CE linked in PR 2650
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2650 changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "melee/lb/lb_00CE.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/lb/lb_00CE.c"). The bot report listed two new matches: main/melee/lb/lb_00CE .sdata2 to 100.00% and lb_8000D148 to 100.00%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-specific history for future lb_00CE matching or regression triage.

source_update_candidates.title: lb_00CE .sdata2 labels localized in PR 2650
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2650 changed lb_00CE .sdata2 symbols at 0x804D7A68-0x804D7AA4 from global lb_804D-address names to local @ labels while retaining float/double sizes and alignment.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future curators distinguish local constant metadata from real global data ownership in this TU.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow evidence requirements for sdata2_order-style float-order helpers
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that sdata2_order-style unused helper functions have historical precedent for forcing float order, but should remain narrow, evidence-backed, and path-specific, with bot/objdiff evidence and symbol metadata supporting the data-order need.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR shows a successful and reviewed tactic, but current standards already caution against broad data-order hacks; curator should decide whether this warrants a clarified exception note.

rejection_notes: Do not promote sdata2_order helpers as generally preferred style; the PR evidence supports only a narrow lb_00CE tactic with a bot report and one review sentence.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the loaded text; the changes-requested review only says "One sec."
Do not claim local build, objdiff, or regression checks beyond the decomp-dev bot report because no such logs are loaded.
Do not treat all address-style lb_804D symbols as wrong; this PR only changed a specific lb_00CE .sdata2 range.
